title: ElasticSearch
author: Priya
content:
What is Elastic search ?

 It is a search and analytics engine that uses semi-structured JSON based database for storing data and performs searching as full text based search by taking the entire search keyword or semantically with vector search.

            It is designed to 

* Store large volumes of data

* Search data very fast

* Analyze data in real time

* Scale horizontally (across multiple servers). if amount of data is huge they are partitioned into small schemas of database having same metadata in different server nodes and the process is known as sharding. 


ElasticSearch features 

- Elastic search cloud is a stateless http based model and can spanned around multiple server nodes for handling large chunks of data.

- Sharding is done in Elasticsearch db to achieve horizontal scaling.

- Incase 1 server is down due to some reason replicas of shards r created in another server which can be used in need when 1 server node is down. 

- Elastic search do parallel processing , it means it can handle large number of users' query parallelly . 

-Any data comes to the cluster gets automatically synced with the database and become available for quick search .

- The response time for answering a query is less than a second and this is why it is fastest form for analytics and search engine that can handle a request in no time.

- If a user request comes in , it is handled by 1 type of elastic cluster node - coordinating node(among all of master node , data node , coordinating node and ingest node) and it will check the user request query in all the relevant shards of multi node(server) database and assemble every response came by querying db and return that to user.

Elastic search application

- For visualization of logs .A large volume of application or system logs can be monitored and visualised.

- Used in banking and fintech sector to identity suspicious activities and generate trigger.

- In live metric tracking. Due to its robust data mechanism any data comes to the elastic search dashboard gets automatically update and refreshed . And we can easily perform live search and analytics in real time.

-. In giving auto suggestion in search results


Traditional Vs Vector search

- In traditional search , searches r made on the exact match as asked in the query. There is no smart message filtering to take out responses similar or close to the query being asked.

eg... if search is for sustainable clothings , it will show all the matches for clothings not specific to eco friendly or green clothings , the relevance is not in the contextual meaning of the world sustainable but search for the exact match of sustainable clothings.

.In advance filtering , U may also see all search results of similar query for those specific type of clothings , clothes with specific label of eco friendly match and also all similar type of clothes well fitting in the query. It will take actual derivative of the world "sustainable" and will compare it with nature , comfort , green clothings , environment friendly and every other likeness it can draw from the word sustainable and will show all such results. This type of intelligent message filtering comes in vector search.

Taking an another representation of traditional and vector search. If supposedly search query has searches for "best pizza restaurant" than a traditional search will pick the keywords "best pizza" , "pizza restaurant" , or just some good pizza outlets and will display results based on that. On the other hand doing the same search with vector would search for best top rated pizza restaurant, some good pizza bars or some of nearest pizza restaurant or something that have good ratings and reviews .It will check and compare search query within its dB and would give the best matching , suitable results as per the query. It actually checks every variables and semantics of the text find its contextual meaning and return all those items matching with the search parameters.


The traditional search failed in many scenarios due to its findings of exact match and uses no smart message search modelling that we can fetch from advance search mechanism like in vector or hybrid search . Due to complexities of handling large volume of user queries with no smart search mechanism , vector search superseded traditional search method. Now , most advance sites using vector or hybrid search approach with which large number of queries could be answered in less time with more clarity .

What is Vector search ?

~ Vectors are numerical representations of words, entities, documents, images or videos.Here, searching's based on certain numeric vectors .When a query comes, data points having some numeric value stored as vector in db r compared with the actual query to find the similarities and fetch all those results which r closely related to search query. It gives more meaningful and accurate representations of items as vectors by taking out the similarity between users query and the stored vectors in the numeral form of vector space.

eg... If users' query is " show low priced red color dress"

Than , this query is passed to elastic search cluster and all those shards having data of "red" , "dress" at "low cost" and all related checkpoints containing dresses which r red , affordable will be retrieved .From all such shards giving those query results will be combined and sent back to user.

Here, Vector search use keywords like minimum price "1000" , color "red" than convert them into data strings having some numeral value and will check every data points into vector space to find all those results having close likelihood by comparing those numerals .So, searching among shards will look for all dress under minimum base price and coloured red.

An internal comparison of users' query from the data points present in the vector space.

Search query -> This sentence is about to ...






ElasticSearch being a good vector db -

It does hybrid search . Look for full text search and vector semantic search .And gives a precise result as per the query in less than a second time.

If search includes -

Text to image search = The text are checked to find its literal meaning and exact connotation of words and is than converted to data string shown as numerical representations (vectors) to derive the actual result by comparing and equating it to numerals stored in vector db.

Image to image search= images converted into its numerical form (vectors), captured their visual characteristics, such as shapes, colors, and textures, analyze pixel data to extract meaningful patterns.By comparing the vectors of query image to those in the database, vector search identifies visually similar results and output that to user.

Pure vector db alone may struggle with advance searching and filtering, enterprise security but Elasticsearch gives best results using vector db along with exact word match and keyword precision.

So, Elasticsearch is not a “pure” vector database — it is a distributed search engine that also provides powerful vector search.

It's a form of hybrid search model that utilizes vector search properties on the get-go.


Some noteworthy key aspects of Elasticsearch 


Horizontal Scaling with database sharding

Parallel Data processing and execution

Vector based search for an accurate response

Handling of large volume of data

Faster execution in milliseconds
